title: Derivative of function and its inverse

concepts.title: Differentiation of Inverse Functions
concepts.conceptBody:
Differentiation of Inverse functions 

Before going to differentiation let us revise some important results related to inverse functions

  • $\text{ta}{{\text{n}}^{-1}}x+\text{ta}{{\text{n}}^{-1}}y=\text{ta}{{\text{n}}^{-1}}\left( \frac{x+y}{1-xy} \right)x>0,y>0,xy<1$
  • $\text{ta}{{\text{n}}^{-1}}x+\text{ta}{{\text{n}}^{-1}}y=\pi +\text{ta}{{\text{n}}^{-1}}\left( \frac{x+y}{1-xy} \right)\text{ }\!\!~\!\!\text{ }{{x}_{1}}>0,y>0,xy>1$
  • $\text{ta}{{\text{n}}^{-1}}x-\text{ta}{{\text{n}}^{-1}}y=\text{ta}{{\text{n}}^{-1}}\left( \frac{x-y}{1+xy} \right)$ if $x>0,y>0$
  • $\text{se}{{\text{c}}^{-1}}\left( \frac{1}{x} \right)=\text{co}{{\text{s}}^{-1}}x$
  • $\operatorname{cosec}^{-1}\left(\frac{1}{x}\right)=\sin ^{-1}(x)$
  • $\text{co}{{\text{t}}^{-1}}\left( \frac{1}{x} \right)=\text{ta}{{\text{n}}^{-1}}x$

We advise to change $\text{se}{{\text{c}}^{-1}}\And \text{cose}{{\text{c}}^{-1}}$ by using above rules to ease out differentiation/

Some substitutions: 

  • $\text{si}{{\text{n}}^{-1}}\left( \frac{2\text{x}}{1+{{\text{x}}^{2}}} \right)=2\text{ta}{{\text{n}}^{-1}}\text{x }\!\!~\!\!\text{ }$ (put $\text{x}=\text{tan}\theta )$
  • $\text{co}{{\text{s}}^{-1}}\left( \frac{1-{{x}^{2}}}{1+{{x}^{2}}} \right)=2\text{ta}{{\text{n}}^{-1}}x\text{ }\!\!~\!\!\text{ }$ (put $x=\text{tan}\theta )$

put the following substitutions for the expression

$\frac{1-\text{x}}{1+\text{x}}\overset{\text{ substitute }}{\mathop{\to }}\,\text{x}=\cos \theta $

${{\text{a}}^{2}}-{{\text{x}}^{2}},\sqrt{{{\text{a}}^{2}}-{{\text{x}}^{2}}}\overset{\text{ substitute }}{\mathop{\to }}\,\text{x}=\frac{\text{a}\cos \theta }{\text{a}\sin \theta }$

${{\text{x}}^{2}}-{{\text{a}}^{2}},\sqrt{{{\text{x}}^{2}}-{{\text{a}}^{2}}}\overset{\text{ substitute }}{\mathop{\to }}\,\text{x}=\text{a}\sec \theta /\text{a}\operatorname{cosec}\theta $

${{\text{x}}^{2}}+{{\text{a}}^{2}},\sqrt{{{\text{x}}^{2}}+{{\text{a}}^{2}}}\overset{\text{ substitute }}{\mathop{\to }}\,\text{x}=\text{a}\tan \theta /\text{a}\cot \theta $
